title: Phlegmariurus terrae-guilelmi
intro: Phlegmariurus terrae-guilelmi, also known as the Guilelmus' ground fern or Guilelmus' resurrection fern, is a species of flowering plant in the lycopodiopsida class of vascular plants, specifically in the Phlegmariurus genus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Phlegmariurus terrae-guilelmi include compounds such as lignans, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phlegmariurus terrae-guilelmi is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Phlegmariurus terrae-guilelmi is classified within the family Lycopodiaceae. This family is part of the class Lycopodiopsida and the division Lycopodiophyta. The order Lycopodiales is also part of its classification. The kingdom Plantae encompasses this species, which is further divided into the kingdom's main categories. This classification reflects Phlegmariurus terrae-guilelmi's evolutionary relationships with other plants.
